purpose: Single governed KPI code registry for CNC Performance Operating System. Existing ANNEX-122 KPIs are retained, legacy metric aliases are mapped to canonical codes, and proposed CNC operating metrics are staged without granting automatic runtime authority.
authority_rule: KpiEngine may auto-register only metrics listed in runtime_calculated_metrics after alias normalization. Governance-only and proposed operating metrics require an approved data contract, owner, formula, evaluation use, review cadence, evidence source, and consequence rule before runtime calculation. A measurement that is not used for approved evaluation must not be named KPI.

annex122_governance_kpis.name: Quote Hit Rate vs Qualified RFQ

proposed_operating_metrics.name: Supplier readiness composite from OTD and incoming quality
Actual cycle time variance vs standard

document_audit.interpretation: The estate has broad KPI language, but only ANNEX-122 governance KPIs, ANNEX-110 dashboard KPIs, gate metrics, runtime metrics, and explicitly approved operational metrics are controlled KPI authority.

performance_governance_audit.interpretation: This is a conservative control audit. Missing local people-governance terms means the document must either rely on ANNEX-127 performance governance or rename the item to operating metric, control metric, gate metric, role measure, or health indicator.

role_measure_policy.rule: JD measures support performance review and OJT but are not official enterprise KPIs unless mapped to this registry.

performance_governance_policy.naming_rule: A measurement may be called KPI only when it has an approved evaluation use. If it is not used for evaluation, call it operating metric, control metric, gate metric, role performance measure, or health indicator.

classification_rule.kpi: Used in a formal scorecard or performance review with owner, cadence, target, evidence, rating method, recognition rule, and corrective-action rule.
classification_rule.operating_metric: Used for shift/day/week control and escalation; may trigger action but not direct reward or discipline.
classification_rule.gate_control_metric: Used to hold/release work, certify readiness, or prove QMS/MES evidence; discipline only applies to verified bypass, falsification, or repeated non-compliance.
classification_rule.role_performance_measure: Used in JD, OJT, competency, or review of role execution; not an enterprise KPI unless mapped to this registry.
classification_rule.health_indicator: Monitored for awareness or trend review; no rating, reward, or discipline.

performance_governance_policy.recognition_rule: Recognition can be monetary or non-monetary, individual or team based, but must use a balanced bundle of controllable measures. Do not reward one metric alone when safety, quality, delivery, or data integrity is weak.
performance_governance_policy.corrective_action_rule: Below-target performance triggers review, coaching, root cause, CAPA, resource escalation, training, or process redesign before any people discipline.
performance_governance_policy.discipline_guardrail: Discipline is allowed only for verified controllable behavior: data falsification, bypassing a required gate, unsafe act, repeated non-compliance after coaching, or refusal to follow a released procedure. Do not punish an uncontrollable outcome caused by material shortage, customer change, machine failure, engineering defect, or management resource decision.
performance_governance_policy.anti_gaming_guardrail: Every KPI used for reward or discipline must have a counter-metric or gate: safety, quality escape, customer impact, evidence integrity, and supervisor/QA review.

metric_governance_schema.rule: Every catalog metric must expose intent, behavior motive, expected result, decision purpose, evaluation use, consequence guardrail, anti-gaming counter metric, and data-contract status. Official KPI means metric_type = kpi and evaluation_use is approved.

metric_governance_defaults.rating_method: RAG plus owner review; use calibration before people consequence.
metric_governance_defaults.recognition_rule: Recognition is allowed only through a balanced bundle and confirmed evidence; never reward one metric while safety, quality, delivery, or data integrity is weak.
metric_governance_defaults.corrective_action_rule: Below-target result triggers owner review, action log, root cause, CAPA/kaizen, training, or resource escalation before people discipline.
metric_governance_defaults.discipline_guardrail: No direct discipline from an outcome metric. Discipline requires verified controllable behavior such as data falsification, bypassing a required gate, unsafe act, repeated non-compliance after coaching, or refusal to follow a released procedure.
metric_governance_defaults.anti_gaming_guardrail: Every rewardable KPI must be paired with a counter-metric or gate for safety, quality escape, customer impact, evidence integrity, and supervisor/QA review.
metric_governance_defaults.controllability_scope: Shared system outcome unless a documented event proves an individual's controllable behavior.

OTD.motive: Drive realistic promise dates, stable dispatch, blocker escalation, and ship-ready discipline.
OTD.expected_result: Higher on-time shipment rate without sacrificing quality, safety, or data integrity.
OTD.decision_purpose: Prioritize recovery, capacity tradeoffs, customer communication, and management review action.
OTD.review_forum: T2/T3 delivery recovery and monthly BSC

COMPLAINT_RATE.strategic_intent: Expose customer escapes and external quality risk.
COMPLAINT_RATE.motive: Prevent trading speed for customer defects and force containment/CAPA on escapes.
COMPLAINT_RATE.expected_result: Lower complaint and customer escape rate with faster containment.
COMPLAINT_RATE.decision_purpose: Escalate customer risk, CAPA, containment, and customer communication.
COMPLAINT_RATE.accountable_owner: QA Manager / Sales and Customer Service Manager
COMPLAINT_RATE.review_forum: management review and customer complaint review

GROSS_MARGIN_JOB_FAMILY.strategic_intent: Separate profitable job families from revenue that consumes constraint capacity.
GROSS_MARGIN_JOB_FAMILY.motive: Drive quote discipline, mix decisions, and engineering/manufacturing cost visibility.
GROSS_MARGIN_JOB_FAMILY.expected_result: Better margin by job family without hiding COPQ, expedite, or rework cost.
GROSS_MARGIN_JOB_FAMILY.decision_purpose: Set pricing, customer mix, process improvement, and constraint allocation priorities.

THROUGHPUT_PER_CONSTRAINT_HOUR.strategic_intent: Maximize value created through the current constraint.
THROUGHPUT_PER_CONSTRAINT_HOUR.motive: Protect bottleneck time and avoid optimizing non-constraint resources.
THROUGHPUT_PER_CONSTRAINT_HOUR.expected_result: More revenue or contribution per constraint hour with stable delivery and quality.
THROUGHPUT_PER_CONSTRAINT_HOUR.decision_purpose: Choose job mix, setup support, fixture/gage/CMM priority, maintenance priority, and outsource decisions.

COPQ.strategic_intent: Make poor quality visible in money, not only defect counts.
COPQ.motive: Reduce rework, scrap, concession, expedite, and customer escape cost.
COPQ.expected_result: Lower cost of poor quality with finance-approved cost buckets.
COPQ.decision_purpose: Prioritize quality improvement, CAPA, tooling, inspection, and supplier actions.

FPY.strategic_intent: Increase right-first-time flow through CNC operations.
FPY.motive: Prevent defects at source instead of improving inspection-only performance.
FPY.expected_result: Higher first-pass yield with lower rework and customer escape.
FPY.decision_purpose: Prioritize process capability, setup control, inspection plan, training, and CAPA.
FPY.review_forum: Lean Daily Management and BSC review

OEE_BOTTLENECK.strategic_intent: See true capacity at the active bottleneck.
OEE_BOTTLENECK.motive: Focus setup, fixture, gage, CMM, maintenance, and support on the constraint first.
OEE_BOTTLENECK.expected_result: Higher available quality capacity at the bottleneck without unsafe shortcuts.
OEE_BOTTLENECK.decision_purpose: Escalate bottleneck losses, reason codes, maintenance, setup support, and DBR priorities.
OEE_BOTTLENECK.accountable_owner: Production Director / Maintenance Manager / Planning Manager

WIP_AGING.strategic_intent: Expose stuck jobs and hidden queue risk before OTD fails.
WIP_AGING.motive: Escalate material, setup, QA, outsource, and engineering blockers instead of releasing more WIP.
WIP_AGING.expected_result: Lower aged WIP and faster blocker resolution.
WIP_AGING.decision_purpose: Set recovery priorities and prevent WIP growth before the constraint.
WIP_AGING.review_forum: Lean Daily Management and constraint review

SETUP_FIRST_PASS.strategic_intent: Make setup ready-to-run before production quantity starts.
SETUP_FIRST_PASS.motive: Prepare program, setup sheet, tools, fixtures, offsets, and gages before first piece.
SETUP_FIRST_PASS.expected_result: Fewer setup loops, prove-out failures, and setup-related scrap.
SETUP_FIRST_PASS.decision_purpose: Improve setup standard work, SMED, pre-run readiness, and training.
SETUP_FIRST_PASS.accountable_owner: Production Director / Engineering Manager / QA Manager
SETUP_FIRST_PASS.review_forum: setup readiness review and BSC review

FAI_FIRST_PASS.strategic_intent: Prevent production release before first article/first piece is proven.
FAI_FIRST_PASS.motive: Align drawing revision, program, setup, gage, and inspection plan before quantity run.
FAI_FIRST_PASS.expected_result: Higher FAI first-pass and fewer loops before production release.
FAI_FIRST_PASS.decision_purpose: Hold/release work, trigger engineering/setup/gage review, and prevent escapes.
FAI_FIRST_PASS.accountable_owner: QA Manager / Production Director / Engineering Manager
FAI_FIRST_PASS.review_forum: QMS/MES gate review and BSC review

REPEAT_NCR_RATE.strategic_intent: Expose recurring failures that CAPA did not eliminate.
REPEAT_NCR_RATE.motive: Close root causes with evidence instead of closing NCRs administratively.
REPEAT_NCR_RATE.expected_result: Lower repeat NCR rate by defect family, process, supplier, and customer.
REPEAT_NCR_RATE.decision_purpose: Escalate systemic issues to CAPA, process redesign, supplier action, or training.

CAPA_EFFECTIVENESS.strategic_intent: Prove CAPA prevents recurrence after verification window.
CAPA_EFFECTIVENESS.motive: Close CAPA by effectiveness evidence, not by due-date compliance alone.
CAPA_EFFECTIVENESS.expected_result: More effective CAPA and fewer recurring nonconformances.
CAPA_EFFECTIVENESS.decision_purpose: Reopen weak CAPA, escalate systemic risk, and allocate improvement resources.

SUPPLIER_READINESS.strategic_intent: Measure whether suppliers and outsource processors are ready for the production plan.
SUPPLIER_READINESS.motive: Balance supplier on-time delivery with usable incoming quality.
SUPPLIER_READINESS.expected_result: Fewer shortages, outsource defects, and plan disruptions caused by supplier readiness gaps.
SUPPLIER_READINESS.decision_purpose: Prioritize supplier escalation, SCAR, sourcing changes, incoming inspection, and material recovery.
SUPPLIER_READINESS.review_cadence: weekly supplier recovery and monthly scorecard

CRITICAL_ROLE_CERT_COVERAGE.strategic_intent: Prevent critical people roles from becoming hidden bottlenecks.
CRITICAL_ROLE_CERT_COVERAGE.motive: Build certified backup for setup, CMM, QC, planner, maintenance, and release roles.
CRITICAL_ROLE_CERT_COVERAGE.expected_result: Stable operations when key people are absent or demand shifts.
CRITICAL_ROLE_CERT_COVERAGE.decision_purpose: Prioritize OJT, cross-training, certification gates, and staffing risk actions.

RECORDABLE_INCIDENT_RATE.strategic_intent: Keep safety as a non-negotiable operating gate.
RECORDABLE_INCIDENT_RATE.motive: Stop unsafe tradeoffs for output, schedule, or OEE.
RECORDABLE_INCIDENT_RATE.expected_result: Fewer recordable incidents and stronger near-miss learning.
RECORDABLE_INCIDENT_RATE.decision_purpose: Block unsafe work, escalate hazards, correct training/PPE/process gaps, and review leadership controls.
RECORDABLE_INCIDENT_RATE.review_cadence: daily incident review and monthly BSC

backend_api_policy.rule: Calculation endpoints are allowed only for runtime_calculated_metrics after alias normalization. Known non-runtime metrics must return catalog coverage and data-contract status, not a fake value.
